Tests the type difference between `a.print.toString` and `b.print.toString` after modification.

# Purpose
This code defines a function [`c`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/06/06-002c>) that creates and returns an object with a method `print`. The `print` method assigns the argument `arg` to a variable `f`, although `f` is not declared within the function, which may lead to unexpected behavior. Two objects, `a` and `b`, are created by calling [`c`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/06/06-002c>) with different arguments. The code then assigns the number `7` to the `toString` property of `a.print` and uses an `assert` statement to check that the type of `a.print.toString` is not the same as the type of `b.print.toString`. This script demonstrates object creation, method assignment, and type checking in JavaScript.

### c<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/06/06-002.c}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/06/06-002.js#L15>)

Creates an object with a `print` method that assigns a given argument to a global variable `f`.
- **Inputs**:
    - `arg`: The argument to assign to the global variable `f` when the `print` method is called.
- **Control Flow**:
    - Create a new object `obj`.
    - Define a `print` method on `obj` that assigns the value of `arg` to a global variable `f`.
    - Return the object `obj`.
- **Output**: An object with a `print` method that assigns the input argument to a global variable `f` when called.
